factors.name: US-Iran Escalation (Geopolitical)
factors.summary: Fresh US strikes on Iran overnight with retaliatory Iranian missile/drone attacks on Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan bases. Oil spiking 3.5%+ to $74.50 WTI, raising energy input costs and uncertainty for cyclical Dow names (CAT, BA, MMM). Traditional risk-off driver, but markets still digesting intensity.

factors.name: China CPI/PPI Divergence
factors.summary: China CPI missed expectations at 1.0% YoY (expected 1.1%, prior 1.2%), signaling weak domestic demand. PPI surged to 4.1% YoY (4-year high), squeezing manufacturer margins. Dow has ~50% international revenue; weak China growth and margin compression headwind for industrials (CAT, DE, GE, HON).

factors.name: Oil Price Rally (War Premium)
factors.summary: Oil +3.5% on Iran strikes, but no major Dow energy weight (CVX minimal in index). Bullish for energy stocks but bearish for airlines, transport, and consumer discretionary due to fuel cost pressures. Net negative for broad Dow given industrial/transport exposure.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: FOMC minutes showed 'a few' participants saw case for rate hike, but inflation concerns now tied to oil spikes (transitory risk). No major scheduled FOMC in immediate window. Lack of clear guidance creates hold pattern.

factors.name: Dow Heavyweight Earnings Cycle
factors.summary: Levi Strauss beat expectations and raised guidance, but shares fell AH (execution vs. sentiment divergence). Lack of major Dow component earnings catalyst in next 24-48h; mixed signals on consumer resilience and industrial demand.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility (Geopolitical Risk-Off)
factors.summary: Implied volatility rising on Iran escalation; futures described as 'steady' (not rallying) despite overnight developments. Risk-off tone limits upside momentum; broad equity appetite muted by 4h -0.05%, 24h +0.11% near-flatness.

factors.name: Dollar Strength (Multinational Translation)
factors.summary: USD/CNY set by PBOC at 6.8036 (slightly weaker CNY), GBP near 1.3400, JPY 162.45 (yen buying on intervention fears). No extreme dollar move; modest cross-currency noise but no major tail wind or head wind for dollar-heavy Dow earners.

factors.name: Credit Spreads & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Harley-Davidson downgraded to junk; Aston Martin creditors signing cooperation pact (distress signals). Credit stress building in leveraged/cyclical names; risk appetite contracting on war premium and China slowdown.

factors.name: Semiconductors (Chip Rally Offsetting)
factors.summary: SK Hynix US ADR 7x oversubscribed, Broadcom-Apple supply deal and Nvidia China H200 purchases lifting Kospi/Nikkei. Offsetting some geopolitical selloff; AI demand structural support. Limited Dow weighting but positive sentiment for tech-correlated players.

factors.name: Near-Term Price Action
factors.summary: 4h change -0.05%, 24h change +0.11% shows index nearly flat despite overnight Iran escalation, China miss, and oil rally. Move is muted; no decisional momentum in either direction despite multiple drivers. Suggests consolidation/uncertainty phase.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between conflicting forces: escalating US-Iran military strikes (oil +3.5%, traditional risk-off) and weak China CPI (growth headwind for industrials) are bearish for a Dow heavy in CAT, BA, MMM, GE. However, chip rally (AI demand) and beat earnings (Levi's) offer modest offset. The near-flat 24h action (±0.11%) despite overnight Iran strikes and China data misses reveals market indecision—investors are neither fleeing nor buying on dips. Geopolitical uncertainty and China slowdown are creating a consolidation box rather than directional commitment.
catalysts_ahead: US Initial Jobless Claims (Thursday this week, per FXStreet mention)
Further US-Iran escalation or de-escalation signals (Trump mentioned Iran 'wants a deal'—watch for next statements)
Oil price volatility on Strait of Hormuz supply fears (ongoing, could swing sentiment)
China economic data follow-up (next monthly PMI/industrial production)
Dow component earnings (major names still to report in mid-July earnings season)
FOMC meeting next week (per analyst mentions of hawkish commentary)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US tensions rapidly de-escalate (Trump deal momentum), risk-off sentiment could reverse sharply and lift equities (Dow +1–2% upside). Conversely, if geopolitical escalates further (Strait of Hormuz closure threat), oil could spike >$80 and trigger broader selloff (Dow -2–3% downside). China stimulus or surprise growth signal would flip the industrial/cyclical bias bullish. Watch FOMC forward guidance next week for any dovish pivot that could support equities despite oil pressure.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (overnight catalysts hitting, but price action flat/consolidating). Avoid chasing—wait for a directional retest: either break above 52,500 on risk-on reversal (Iran deal/chip momentum), or break below 52,100 on geopolitical escalation, before committing. Current 4h/24h flatness suggests market waiting for clarity; low-confidence zone for aggressive entries.
